programmes.show_description: The staff are up early to greet a new arrival at Hatley Station - another arrival later in the day is less welcome... S1 E7: Past Love
When a shady character from the future arrives at Gary's shop to close the time portal, Gary begins to contemplate where his future lies. S6 E6: Just in Time
When the hapless bumbler Mr. Bean goes back to school, he scares a boy in the chemistry lab and the nudes in the art class cause him to look at the floor. E11: Back to School, Mr. Bean
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. When Sam responds to a stabbing, he finds it's at the converted mill where he lives in 2006. Starring John Simm. S1 E3
Sandra enjoys a passionate week with Rex while her mums in hospital, but normality soon returns. Beryl hears from runaway Gwyn, and Mrs Hennessey seeks divine lottery tips. S10 E6: Out of Hospital
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
David and Pamela are married with Malcolm as best man. Susan picks up a very unusual wedding gift. S3 E6: Wedding
Sharon, Tracey and Dorien all lose their jobs and the resulting financial crisis leads to a huge row - some home truths are revealed and the three of them hit rock bottom. S10 E7: Back To Zero
Mr. Bean turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment as he visits a department store and gets carried away. E2: The Return of Mr. Bean
David is carried away by the throb of guitars, the drone of the cicadas, the scent of the bougainvillaea, and most of all by Linda. S1 E3: Praying Mantis

The workers at the pickle factory go on strike when told they cannot use the new executive washroom. S3 E4: The Power Behind The Throne
The couples join a coach trip to the mountains where David buys Linda a gypsy shawl she has admired - when Amy finds that she has lost her money, David sells his watch. S1 E4: Spanish Lace
The staff discover that a libellous word has been painted on Lord Meldrum's car - who was the phantom signwriter? S1 E1 The Phantom Signwriter
Rigsby finally pops the question to Miss Jones with his decree absolute at long last in hand, but will she accept? S4 E6: Come On In The Water's Lovely
Wolfie is shell-shocked to be given a job despite his best efforts otherwise, but Dad is delighted that Yeti is joining the honest working classes. S2 E4: Working Class Hero
To Mrs Boswell's horror, Billy and Julie decide to have their baby christened in a Protestant church. S3 E1
To lift their spirits, Sandra and Beryl hit the town, where a night of drinks, surprises and misjudged optimism promises more chaos than comfort. S10 E4: On the Town
May is keen to make a career move to a bigger station, but both Jack and Cecil are set against her going and both try to apply for a transfer to be near her. S1 E8: Job Opportunity
The staff discover that a libellous word has been painted on Lord Meldrum's car - who was the phantom signwriter? S1 E1 The Phantom Signwriter
Larry gives girls fake names; the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name
Au pair Danielle brings her employers' baby to the college and leaves him in the canteen. When the class ends, he is gone. S3 E2: Who Loves Ya Baby?
Alf and Arthur enjoy a pint discussing the free society. Alf reckons you can have a free society - but only with control and discipline. S2 E4
To lift their spirits, Sandra and Beryl hit the town, where a night of drinks, surprises and misjudged optimism promises more chaos than comfort. S10 E4: On the Town
To Mrs Boswell's horror, Billy and Julie decide to have their baby christened in a Protestant church. S3 E1
To make room in the lock-up, Arthur and Ray swap its contents for a fruit machine filled with stolen money.
May is keen to make a career move to a bigger station, but both Jack and Cecil are set against her going and both try to apply for a transfer to be near her. S1 E8: Job Opportunity
Gary thinks that Yvonne is having an affair, so he asks Ron to spy on her. Plus, Gary and Phoebe join a concert troupe in Belgium, but their efforts are quickly thwarted. S6 E7: How I Won the War
It turns our that Mr. Bean isn't very good at looking after things in a laundrette, and he later has difficulty keeping control of his ball during a round of mini golf. E12: Tee Off, Mr. Bean
Sam uncovers corruption in CID when he takes on a local crime boss. When the crime boss takes steps to stop Sam's investigation, will Gene do what is right? Starring John Simm. S1 E4
Reunited at last, Beryl and Sandra clash over their mothers when Beryl shatters Sandra's Rex-fuelled fantasy. Hurt and angry, Beryl moves out, threatening their fragile friendship. S10 E7: Moving Out
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
This is where Brenda meets Malcolm - they are both very different and this causes problems, but they find themselves drawn to one another. S1 E1: Meeting
Sharon and Tracey get a glimpse of a possible future when they see two elderly sisters in the supermarket, and Dorien's court case comes to a conclusion. S10 E8: You Can't Always Get What You Want
Mr. Bean goes to a swimming pool but struggles with the diving board, attempts to leave a car park in style and demonstrates some sandwich-making. E3: The Curse of Mr. Bean

Eli is planning a weekend away while Nellie wants to decorate the cellar, but an error means that Eli and Nellie are stuck together for the weekend. S3 E5: Getting To Know You
Amy's suspicions are further aroused by David and Linda's midnight swim, and Robert also suspects that a romance is in the air. S1 E5: Bedroom Farce
The Honourable Teddy creeps upstairs every night and tries to get into Ivy's room, and Lady Lavender is bent on giving away her shares of the family fortune to the servants. S1 E2 The Deed of Gift
Rigsby is looking for another lodger - one of his current lodgers, Miss Jones, is keen for her acquaintance Philip to move in because she is attracted to him. S1 E1: The New Lodger
A foreign revolutionary arrives at the house asking after Wolfie and talking about financial support. S2 E5: Spanish Fly
It's Grandad's 75th birthday, and Jack and Adrian buy him a present which turns out to be more valuable than they think. S3 E2
Beryl pushes Sandra to spend a week with Rex while her mums in hospital. As Sandra gambles on love, Gwyn abruptly goes on the run. S10 E5: Going Into Hospital
Jack and May have problems with their teenage daughter, and it's dance night and Ethel is excited about hoping to find love at last. S1 E9: Sleeping Around
The Honourable Teddy creeps upstairs every night and tries to get into Ivy's room, and Lady Lavender is bent on giving away her shares of the family fortune to the servants. S1 E2 The Deed of Gift
Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening while Jo runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap
Mr. Brown breaks his leg after taking Juan to the hospital, and the Sister on duty accidentally informs the school he has died. S3 E3: No Flowers by Request
With the aid of a jacket full of battle ribbons and Else's chair, Alf and Arthur find themselves in the money. S2 E5

Arthur falls out with Dave when a member of their boyhood gang reappears on the scene. S9 E6: Gone With the Winchester
